# UC-13.6.12 · Grafana Explore Query Volume and Slow Query Detection

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Performance, Capacity &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch how often people use the fast ad-hoc charting screen and how long those lookups take. When someone accidentally asks for far too much data at once, we spot it early so shared systems keep breathing and teams get a friendly nudge instead of a surprise outage.*

---

## Description

Monitors Grafana Explore sessions where analysts issue ad-hoc queries that bypass dashboard caching and can hammer shared backends with wide time ranges, high-cardinality label filters, or expensive TraceQL and LogQL patterns. Unlike UC-13.6.1 and UC-13.6.2, which center on datasource transport errors and failed queries, this control measures successful-but-heavy traffic: volume per authenticated user, per datasource UID, and inferred window length, then highlights slow tails above thirty seconds that often precede Loki, Tempo, Prometheus, or Splunk saturation. It distinguishes Explore-driven load from scheduled panel refreshes, supports FinOps attribution for capacity planning, and pairs Grafana logs with histogram metrics, backend slow logs, host Performance summaries, and Splunk audit timings when Splunk backs Explore. Sibling UC-13.6.3 covers alert evaluation, UC-13.6.5 covers login audit, UC-13.6.10 covers RBAC drift, UC-13.6.11 covers IdP team sync, UC-13.6.13 covers notifications, UC-13.6.14 covers provisioning drift, and UC-13.6.15 covers Loki cardinality budgets—none replace interactive Explore abuse or coaching signals.

## Value

Platform and observability leaders prevent surprise datastore outages by seeing Explore storms before dashboards turn red: they gain a per-user ledger that ties Grafana proxy latency histograms to datasource slow logs, aligns Splunk-side search duration when Splunk is queried through Grafana, and quantifies how often teams exceed negotiated Explore quotas. FinOps partners receive defensible attribution for heavy tenants, while reliability engineers receive early warning when slow tails concentrate on a single UID or org during incidents. Training teams use the same telemetry to coach safer time-range habits without waiting for vendor bills or manual Grafana screenshots.

## Implementation

Ingest Grafana server and audit logs with Explore path markers, land Prometheus Grafana metrics into a metrics index for mstats, forward Loki, Tempo, SQL slow logs, and Splunk audit lines, publish grafana_user_inventory.csv with explore_quota_per_hour and deploy_freeze_until, accelerate CIM Performance for the tstats arm, save observability_uc_13_6_12_grafana_explore_volume_slow_query every ten to fifteen minutes over four hours, alert on CRITICAL or HIGH severity, and route to platform observability with FinOps cc lists.

## Evidence

Saved search observability_uc_13_6_12_grafana_explore_volume_slow_query with ten-to-fifteen-minute schedule; observability index dashboards with drilldowns to multisearch lanes; weekly CSV exports of user_login and datasource_uid attribution archived to restricted indexes.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In lab Grafana, a test user in grafana_user_inventory.csv with a low explore_quota_per_hour issues thirty Explore queries within one minute against Loki and then runs a sixty-second wildcard query across a large interval; within two scheduled windows observability_uc_13_6_12_grafana_explore_volume_slow_query emits HIGH or CRITICAL with non-null recommended_action and matching datasource_uid rows.

### Negative scenario

After the session ends and only scheduled dashboard refreshes occur without Explore paths for one hour, CRITICAL rows return zero, HIGH rows clear, and severities remain LOW or INFO-equivalent for the test user when deploy_freeze_until is unset.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with observability platform engineers who operate Grafana OSS and Grafana Enterprise fleets, site reliability engineers who defend shared datasources such as Loki, Tempo, Prometheus, and Splunk, FinOps partners who translate query storms into chargeback narratives, and training leads who coach analysts on safe exploration patterns. This use case isolates Grafana Explore behavior where ad-hoc queries bypass dashboard refresh caches, open direct WebSocket or HTTP proxy paths to backends, and amplify cardinality or time-range mistakes into fleet-wide saturation. It is deliberately not UC-13.6.1 or UC-13.6.2 datasource health and query error accounting focused on failing requests and transport faults, not UC-13.6.3 unified alerting evaluation drift, not UC-13.6.4 plugin catalog skew, not UC-13.6.5 login and session audit ratios, not UC-13.6.6 annotation storms, not UC-13.6.7 dashboard version churn, not UC-13.6.8 API token lifecycle governance, not UC-13.6.9 image renderer timeouts, not UC-13.6.10 RBAC permission drift, not UC-13.6.11 LDAP OAuth team sync failures, not UC-13.6.13 notification delivery failures, not UC-13.6.14 provisioned configuration drift, and not UC-13.6.15 Loki cardinality budgeting. The unique angle is Explore-mode telemetry versus scheduled dashboard queries: attribution of volume and latency to individual practitioners, datasource UIDs, inferred time windows, and organizational quotas for capacity planning.

Architecture recap: the Explore UI issues interactive queries through Grafana core, often hitting `/api/ds/query` or datasource proxy routes that fan out to Loki queriers, Tempo query-frontends, Prometheus or Mimir query paths, Elasticsearch, ClickHouse, PostgreSQL, MySQL, or Splunk when configured as a datasource. Grafana 10 and 11 add richer instrumentation: server logs with request identifiers, optional usage statistics reporting, audit events for privileged paths, Prometheus exposition including `grafana_api_dataproxy_request_all_milliseconds` and `grafana_api_response_status_total`, OpenTelemetry traces when collectors wrap Grafana, and Grafana Live channels that behave like long-lived subscriptions rather than point queries. Splunk-side ingestion should preserve `from` and `to` parameters when reverse proxies log query strings, redact literal query text into hashes for privacy, and retain datasource UIDs stable enough to join inventory.

Operational prerequisites include Splunk Add-on for Grafana or equivalent props and transforms that normalize `grafana:server`, `grafana:log`, and `grafana:audit` sourcetypes; a metrics pipeline landing Prometheus scrape of Grafana `/metrics` into `_metrics` or a dedicated metrics index compatible with `mstats`; datasource slow-query feeds such as Loki querier logs, Tempo TraceQL timings, PostgreSQL or MySQL slow logs, Splunk DB Connect diagnostics, or warehouse query logs when Explore targets SQL; and Splunk audit or search-head access logs when Splunk itself is the Grafana datasource so ad-hoc SPL volume correlates with Explore sessions. Publish `lookups/grafana_user_inventory.csv` with columns `user_login`, `user_email`, `team`, `role`, `explore_quota_per_hour`, `allowed_datasources`, and `deploy_freeze_until` so detections understand per-person Explore budgets and change-freeze windows that legitimately downgrade paging.

Risk briefing: Explore is the correct tool during incidents; blind suppression causes missed abuse. Tune severities using `deploy_freeze_until`, team tags that mark incident command roles, and FinOps dashboards instead of muting the pipeline. Legal review should approve retention of usernames tied to query metadata in regulated industries.

Differentiation recap: Explore volume, slow-query tails, per-user attribution, multisource correlation with backend slow logs and Grafana histogram metrics, not generic datasource error rates alone, not alerting rule health, not permission drift, not notification retries.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane A, Grafana server and audit logs: enable info-level logging for HTTP, datasource proxy, and query subsystems. Ship `/var/log/grafana/grafana.log` or container stdout to `index=grafana` with `sourcetype=grafana:server` or `grafana:log` per your add-on. Preserve fields for authenticated user, org, datasource UID, request duration when emitted, status codes, and paths that include `/explore` referrers or `/api/ds/query`. Enable Grafana audit logging for security-relevant query or datasource access when compliance requires non-repudiation.

Lane B, Prometheus metrics from Grafana: scrape `http://grafana:3000/metrics` with a Prometheus agent or OpenTelemetry Collector Prometheus receiver. Forward histograms such as `grafana_api_dataproxy_request_all_milliseconds` and counters like `grafana_api_response_status_total` into Splunk metrics indexes. These series provide service-level latency even when textual logs truncate.

Lane C, datasource backend slow logs: forward Loki querier logs with query latency, Tempo query-frontend logs for TraceQL duration, PostgreSQL `log_min_duration_statement` lines, MySQL slow query logs, Splunk DB Connect query telemetry, and warehouse ODBC or JDBC traces into `index=observability` or domain indexes with clear sourcetypes. Normalize client identities when Explore proxies hide end users so Splunk can still correlate bursts.

Lane D, Splunk as datasource: when Grafana runs SPL through a Splunk datasource, mirror `audittrail` or `splunkd_access` completion events with `total_run_time_ms` to show cross-platform cost. Tag internal service accounts used by automation separately from humans in inventory.

Lane CIM Performance: accelerate the Performance data model on hosts that back Grafana or datasource clusters. The `tstats` arm supplies CPU load correlation for saturation triage when Explore spikes coincide with node pressure.

Knowledge object: maintain `grafana_user_inventory.csv` keyed on `user_login` lowercase. Refresh weekly or on HR tickets. `explore_quota_per_hour` encodes the allowed interactive query count used as a burst reference inside `streamstats` windows. `allowed_datasources` lists UID prefixes permitted for each role; optional future enhancement filters unauthorized datasource attempts in a sibling detection.

Example validation queries after enabling inputs:

index=grafana sourcetype IN ("grafana:log","grafana:server") "/explore" OR "/api/ds/query" earliest=-1h

| mstats avg(_value) WHERE index=_metrics metric_name=grafana_api_dataproxy_request_all_milliseconds earliest=-1h BY host

index=observability sourcetype=loki:query earliest=-1h

Confirm field extractions populate `user_login`, `datasource_uid`, and millisecond latency fields before scheduling detection.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL exactly as the top-level `spl` field in this JSON document under the saved search name `observability_uc_13_6_12_grafana_explore_volume_slow_query`. Schedule every ten or fifteen minutes with `dispatch.earliest_time=-4h@h` and `dispatch.latest_time=now` for production. Alert when `severity` is `CRITICAL` or `HIGH` after deduplication keyed on `user_login`, `datasource_uid`, and a fifteen-minute bucket. Include `recommended_action`, `query_count`, `org_p99_query_ms`, and `allowed_datasources` in pager narratives so responders open Explore coaching or datasource protection runbooks without rerunning the entire multisearch during a bridge.

Pipeline narrative: `multisearch` fans five arms so a silent Grafana log rotation cannot hide a Prometheus histogram spike or a Loki slow-query storm. The first arm isolates Explore-like traffic using path and substring heuristics on raw logs. The second arm uses `mstats` against `grafana_api_dataproxy_request_all_milliseconds` for aggregate proxy latency. The third arm ingests datasource slow logs for ground truth on expensive queries. The fourth arm uses `tstats` on the Performance model to correlate host pressure. The fifth arm pulls Splunk audit timings when Splunk is queried through Grafana. After fusion, `coalesce(user_login,username,user_email)` stabilizes actor naming across exporters. `join` with `inputlookup grafana_user_inventory.csv` attaches quotas and freeze metadata. `streamstats time_window=15m` measures trailing Explore event rate per user. `eventstats` computes org-wide and per-user `p99` latency tiers. `case()` encodes CRITICAL when simultaneous quota burst and slow org tail occur, HIGH when either triggers, MEDIUM for incident-command teams during bridges, and LOW during deploy freezes.

Fenced SPL must match the top-level `spl` field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.6.12 Grafana Explore slow-query and volume attribution multisearch grafana_logs mstats loki_slow perf_tstats splunk_audit join grafana_user_inventory. Tunables: earliest=-4h@h slow_ms=30000")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=grafana (sourcetype=grafana:server OR sourcetype=grafana:log OR sourcetype="grafana:server:log" OR sourcetype=grafana:audit) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      ("/explore" OR Explore OR "/api/ds/query" OR "dataproxy" OR datasource_proxy OR "/api/datasources/proxy" OR "ds/query")
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | eval user_login=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user,userName,user_name,login,Username,""))))
      | eval username=user_login
      | eval user_email=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(email,userEmail,user_email,""))))
      | eval datasource_uid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(datasource_uid,ds_uid,datasourceUid,dsUid,"ds_unknown"))))
      | eval query_ms_raw=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(duration_ms,elapsed_ms,request_duration_ms,latency_ms,"0"))),10)
      | eval query_ms=if(query_ms_raw>0,query_ms_raw,null())
      | eval time_range_sec=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(range_seconds,time_range_sec,window_sec,"0"))),10)
      | eval graf_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,instance,server_name,"gf_host"))))
      | eval lane="grafana_explore_pipeline"
      | fields _time lane user_login username user_email datasource_uid query_ms graf_host time_range_sec ]
    [ | mstats avg(_value) AS query_ms WHERE index=_metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now span=5m metric_name=grafana_api_dataproxy_request_all_milliseconds BY host
      | eval user_login="grafana_dataproxy_service"
      | eval username="grafana_dataproxy_service"
      | eval user_email="metrics_only"
      | eval datasource_uid="prometheus_dataproxy_histogram"
      | eval lane="graf_mstats_dataproxy"
      | eval graf_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,"metrics_agg"))))
      | eval time_range_sec=300
      | fields _time lane user_login username user_email datasource_uid query_ms graf_host time_range_sec ]
    [ search (index=observability OR index=loki OR index=main) (sourcetype=loki:query OR sourcetype=loki:querier OR sourcetype="postgresql:slow" OR sourcetype="mysql:slow" OR sourcetype=db_connect_slow)
      earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      (slow OR duration OR "query time" OR "execution time")
      | eval user_login=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user,org_user,client_id,"ds_client"))))
      | eval username=user_login
      | eval user_email=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user_email,"backend_correlation"))))
      | eval datasource_uid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,datasource,loki_tenant,"loki_backend"))))
      | eval query_ms=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(query_latency_ms,slow_ms,query_duration_ms,"0"))),10)
      | eval lane="datasource_backend_slowlog"
      | eval graf_host="backend_observed"
      | eval time_range_sec=0
      | fields _time lane user_login username user_email datasource_uid query_ms graf_host time_range_sec ]
    [ | tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_ld FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
      | rename Performance.host AS graf_host
      | eval graf_host=lower(trim(toString(graf_host)))
      | eval user_login="perf_correlation_row"
      | eval username="perf_correlation_row"
      | eval user_email="cim_performance"
      | eval datasource_uid=strcat("perf_host:",graf_host)
      | eval query_ms=cpu_ld
      | eval lane="cim_perf_enrichment"
      | eval time_range_sec=0
      | fields _time lane user_login username user_email datasource_uid query_ms graf_host time_range_sec ]
    [ search (index=_audit OR index=security OR index=main) (sourcetype=audittrail OR sourcetype=splunkd_access OR sourcetype="splunk:audit")
      earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      (search_id=* OR action=search OR info=completed)
      | eval user_login=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user,"splunk_search_actor"))))
      | eval username=user_login
      | eval user_email=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user_email,"splunk_contact"))))
      | eval datasource_uid="splunk_as_grafana_datasource"
      | eval query_ms=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(total_run_time_ms,exec_time_ms,"0"))),10)
      | eval lane="splunk_datasource_audit_arm"
      | eval graf_host="splunk_search_tier"
      | eval time_range_sec=0
      | fields _time lane user_login username user_email datasource_uid query_ms graf_host time_range_sec ]
| eval user_login=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user_login,username,user_email,"unknown_explore_actor"))))
| join type=left max=0 user_login
    [| inputlookup grafana_user_inventory.csv
     | eval user_login=lower(trim(toString(user_login)))
     | fields user_login user_email team role explore_quota_per_hour allowed_datasources deploy_freeze_until]
| eval explore_quota_per_hour=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(explore_quota_per_hour,"120"))),10)
| eval deploy_freeze_until=trim(toString(coalesce(deploy_freeze_until,"1970-01-01")))
| eval freeze_epoch=strptime(deploy_freeze_until,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval in_deploy_freeze=if(isnotnull(freeze_epoch)==1 AND now()<freeze_epoch,1,0)
| eval quota_burst_line=explore_quota_per_hour*5/4
| streamstats time_window=15m global=f count AS query_count BY user_login
| eventstats p99(query_ms) AS org_p99_query_ms
| eventstats p99(query_ms) AS user_p99_query_ms BY user_login
| eval incident_window=if(match(team,"incident|war-room|bridge|command") OR match(role,"incident|ic|commander"),1,0)
| eval severity=case(
    in_deploy_freeze==1, "LOW",
    query_count>quota_burst_line AND org_p99_query_ms>30000, "CRITICAL",
    query_count>quota_burst_line OR org_p99_query_ms>30000 OR user_p99_query_ms>30000, "HIGH",
    incident_window==1 AND (query_count>8 OR coalesce(query_ms,0)>15000), "MEDIUM",
    true(), "LOW")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="CRITICAL", "page_platform_throttle_explore_notify_user_cap_datasource",
    severity=="HIGH", "open_bridge_review_queries_and_datasource_saturation",
    severity=="MEDIUM", "coach_user_on_time_range_and_label_cardinality",
    true(), "record_for_weekly_capacity_review")
| table _time user_login team role lane datasource_uid query_count query_ms org_p99_query_ms user_p99_query_ms explore_quota_per_hour severity recommended_action graf_host allowed_datasources deploy_freeze_until
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

[observability_uc_13_6_12_grafana_explore_volume_slow_query_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
action.email = 1
action.email.to = platform-observability-oncall@example.com
action.email.subject = Grafana Explore volume $result.severity$ user=$result.user_login$ ds=$result.datasource_uid$
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_6_12_grafana_explore_volume_slow_query | where severity IN ("CRITICAL","HIGH")

Performance guidance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above targets, summarize each arm hourly into a summary index keyed on `user_login` and `datasource_uid`, widen detection windows on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations.

### Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in Grafana Explore itself: replay the same user session window, capture the query inspector timings, and compare datasource UIDs to Splunk rows. When Grafana shows sub-second latency but Loki slow logs show multi-minute scans, reconcile clock skew and verify you attribute the querier log line to the same org and tenant labels Grafana used.

Histogram cross-check: compare `mstats` medians from `grafana_api_dataproxy_request_all_milliseconds` to Grafana Explore network timings for a test user. Disagreements often mean additional reverse-proxy buffering or missing exemplar labels.

Synthetic positive: in lab, sign in as a test user in `grafana_user_inventory.csv` with a low `explore_quota_per_hour`, issue thirty small Loki queries within one minute from Explore, confirm `query_count` crosses the burst line, then issue a sixty-second wildcard regex query across a large interval and confirm `org_p99_query_ms` or per-user `p99` crosses thirty seconds when backend logs corroborate.

Synthetic slow without volume: run a single Prometheus subquery with a six-hour range and one-minute step from Explore, confirm HIGH or MEDIUM severity with coaching narrative rather than CRITICAL when quota is untouched.

Sustained rate: run a controlled one-hour exploration session at the upper bound of policy and confirm alerts respect `deploy_freeze_until` when you set a future freeze date for the test user.

Negative test: after sessions stop, confirm CRITICAL clears across six consecutive schedules and that LOW severity dominates when only dashboard refreshes occur without Explore paths.

FinOps validation: export weekly CSVs of `user_login`, `query_count`, and `datasource_uid` to finance partners and verify chargeback keys match their models.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case A — Site reliability engineers run legitimately slow queries during incident investigation: tag teams as incident command in inventory, rely on MEDIUM tier coaching, and compare to change calendars before throttling.

Case B — Prometheus subquery patterns such as a six-hour range with one-minute step produce long server-side work without malicious intent: document PromQL templates, teach range-step tradeoffs, and suppress CRITICAL when `datasource_uid` maps to Prometheus and query text hashes match approved incident macros.

Case C — Tempo TraceQL queries span huge windows during outage triage: pair Grafana rows with Tempo query-frontend logs, validate TraceQL filters, and widen time pickers only after narrowing service or trace ID when possible.

Case D — Grafana Live WebSocket channels remain open for streaming dashboards; durations may exceed thirty seconds by design: exclude `lane` values tied to Live telemetry or label those sessions separately in future revisions.

Case E — Synthetic monitors or golden signals that benchmark Explore paths generate periodic slow rows: map service accounts in inventory with high quotas and annotate automation in dashboards.

Case F — PostgreSQL datasource cold caches after restart make first-touch queries slow: correlate `cim_perf_enrichment` CPU and memory with database restart events before blaming analysts.

Case G — Splunk realtime searches initiated through Grafana are slow by definition: downgrade severity when `splunk_datasource_audit_arm` rows show `info=completed` with expected realtime markers and no quota breach.

Case H — Training sandboxes encourage unconstrained exploration: route those orgs to non-production indexes or set generous `explore_quota_per_hour` with cost-center tags instead of production paging.

Case I — Root-cause analysis legitimately requires wide time ranges: require incident_bridge tag on teams and use MEDIUM tier guidance rather than account suspension.

Case J — Loki pattern filters with broad regex during firefighting mimic abuse: verify label selectors tightened after first pass and coach on line filter order.

Case K — Dashboard panel edit previews can look like Explore in logs when developers test queries inline: distinguish via `panelId` versus Explore-only referrers in parser refinements and document the difference for auditors.

Dashboard layout: publish per-user Explore volume trends, per-datasource slow-query top-N, FinOps attribution tiles, and drilldowns to raw five-arm evidence.

Runbook linkage: connect `recommended_action` tokens to articles on Explore guardrails, datasource rate limits, Loki query splitting, Tempo TraceQL efficiency, Splunk search optimization, and PostgreSQL tuning.

Governance: quarterly review `grafana_user_inventory.csv` quotas with team leads so CRITICAL thresholds reflect real capacity rather than stale numbers.

Escalation: page platform observability when CRITICAL rows coincide with datasource saturation metrics on backends; page application owners only after platform confirms healthy infrastructure.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the `spl` field exactly; multisearch lists five arms including Grafana logs, `mstats`, backend slow logs, Performance `tstats`, and Splunk audit; `coalesce`, `join`, `inputlookup`, `streamstats`, `eventstats`, and `case` appear; closing `table` lists sixteen analyst-visible columns.

## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.6.12 Grafana Explore slow-query and volume attribution multisearch grafana_logs mstats loki_slow perf_tstats splunk_audit join grafana_user_inventory. Tunables: earliest=-4h@h slow_ms=30000")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=grafana (sourcetype=grafana:server OR sourcetype=grafana:log OR sourcetype="grafana:server:log" OR sourcetype=grafana:audit) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      ("/explore" OR Explore OR "/api/ds/query" OR "dataproxy" OR datasource_proxy OR "/api/datasources/proxy" OR "ds/query")
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | eval user_login=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user,userName,user_name,login,Username,""))))
      | eval username=user_login
      | eval user_email=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(email,userEmail,user_email,""))))
      | eval datasource_uid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(datasource_uid,ds_uid,datasourceUid,dsUid,"ds_unknown"))))
      | eval query_ms_raw=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(duration_ms,elapsed_ms,request_duration_ms,latency_ms,"0"))),10)
      | eval query_ms=if(query_ms_raw>0,query_ms_raw,null())
      | eval time_range_sec=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(range_seconds,time_range_sec,window_sec,"0"))),10)
      | eval graf_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,instance,server_name,"gf_host"))))
      | eval lane="grafana_explore_pipeline"
      | fields _time lane user_login username user_email datasource_uid query_ms graf_host time_range_sec ]
    [ | mstats avg(_value) AS query_ms WHERE index=_metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now span=5m metric_name=grafana_api_dataproxy_request_all_milliseconds BY host
      | eval user_login="grafana_dataproxy_service"
      | eval username="grafana_dataproxy_service"
      | eval user_email="metrics_only"
      | eval datasource_uid="prometheus_dataproxy_histogram"
      | eval lane="graf_mstats_dataproxy"
      | eval graf_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,"metrics_agg"))))
      | eval time_range_sec=300
      | fields _time lane user_login username user_email datasource_uid query_ms graf_host time_range_sec ]
    [ search (index=observability OR index=loki OR index=main) (sourcetype=loki:query OR sourcetype=loki:querier OR sourcetype="postgresql:slow" OR sourcetype="mysql:slow" OR sourcetype=db_connect_slow)
      earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      (slow OR duration OR "query time" OR "execution time")
      | eval user_login=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user,org_user,client_id,"ds_client"))))
      | eval username=user_login
      | eval user_email=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user_email,"backend_correlation"))))
      | eval datasource_uid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,datasource,loki_tenant,"loki_backend"))))
      | eval query_ms=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(query_latency_ms,slow_ms,query_duration_ms,"0"))),10)
      | eval lane="datasource_backend_slowlog"
      | eval graf_host="backend_observed"
      | eval time_range_sec=0
      | fields _time lane user_login username user_email datasource_uid query_ms graf_host time_range_sec ]
    [ | tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_ld FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
      | rename Performance.host AS graf_host
      | eval graf_host=lower(trim(toString(graf_host)))
      | eval user_login="perf_correlation_row"
      | eval username="perf_correlation_row"
      | eval user_email="cim_performance"
      | eval datasource_uid=strcat("perf_host:",graf_host)
      | eval query_ms=cpu_ld
      | eval lane="cim_perf_enrichment"
      | eval time_range_sec=0
      | fields _time lane user_login username user_email datasource_uid query_ms graf_host time_range_sec ]
    [ search (index=_audit OR index=security OR index=main) (sourcetype=audittrail OR sourcetype=splunkd_access OR sourcetype="splunk:audit")
      earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      (search_id=* OR action=search OR info=completed)
      | eval user_login=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user,"splunk_search_actor"))))
      | eval username=user_login
      | eval user_email=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user_email,"splunk_contact"))))
      | eval datasource_uid="splunk_as_grafana_datasource"
      | eval query_ms=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(total_run_time_ms,exec_time_ms,"0"))),10)
      | eval lane="splunk_datasource_audit_arm"
      | eval graf_host="splunk_search_tier"
      | eval time_range_sec=0
      | fields _time lane user_login username user_email datasource_uid query_ms graf_host time_range_sec ]
| eval user_login=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user_login,username,user_email,"unknown_explore_actor"))))
| join type=left max=0 user_login
    [| inputlookup grafana_user_inventory.csv
     | eval user_login=lower(trim(toString(user_login)))
     | fields user_login user_email team role explore_quota_per_hour allowed_datasources deploy_freeze_until]
| eval explore_quota_per_hour=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(explore_quota_per_hour,"120"))),10)
| eval deploy_freeze_until=trim(toString(coalesce(deploy_freeze_until,"1970-01-01")))
| eval freeze_epoch=strptime(deploy_freeze_until,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval in_deploy_freeze=if(isnotnull(freeze_epoch)==1 AND now()<freeze_epoch,1,0)
| eval quota_burst_line=explore_quota_per_hour*5/4
| streamstats time_window=15m global=f count AS query_count BY user_login
| eventstats p99(query_ms) AS org_p99_query_ms
| eventstats p99(query_ms) AS user_p99_query_ms BY user_login
| eval incident_window=if(match(team,"incident|war-room|bridge|command") OR match(role,"incident|ic|commander"),1,0)
| eval severity=case(
    in_deploy_freeze==1, "LOW",
    query_count>quota_burst_line AND org_p99_query_ms>30000, "CRITICAL",
    query_count>quota_burst_line OR org_p99_query_ms>30000 OR user_p99_query_ms>30000, "HIGH",
    incident_window==1 AND (query_count>8 OR coalesce(query_ms,0)>15000), "MEDIUM",
    true(), "LOW")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="CRITICAL", "page_platform_throttle_explore_notify_user_cap_datasource",
    severity=="HIGH", "open_bridge_review_queries_and_datasource_saturation",
    severity=="MEDIUM", "coach_user_on_time_range_and_label_cardinality",
    true(), "record_for_weekly_capacity_review")
| table _time user_login team role lane datasource_uid query_count query_ms org_p99_query_ms user_p99_query_ms explore_quota_per_hour severity recommended_action graf_host allowed_datasources deploy_freeze_until
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_ld FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS explore_backend_host
| table explore_backend_host cpu_ld
```

## Visualization

Timechart of query_count by user_login, heatmap of datasource_uid slow tails, single-value tiles for org_p99_query_ms, severity-colored master table with drilldowns to multisearch lanes, and FinOps stack charts for quota utilization.

## Known False Positives

Site reliability engineers deliberately run long LogQL, PromQL, TraceQL, or SQL scans during incident bridges; correlate MEDIUM severities with incident-tagged teams instead of treating them as hostile abuse. Data science office hours and quarterly exploration workshops can exceed hourly quotas without impacting production when sandboxes point to sampled indexes; move those users to training inventory tiers. Root-cause analysis windows legitimately span days of data; pair wide ranges with change tickets and incident IDs before throttling accounts. Synthetic probes that benchmark Explore paths from headless browsers emit predictable slow rows; label automation service accounts in grafana_user_inventory.csv with dedicated quotas. Grafana Live streaming channels may remain open indefinitely; durations there are not comparable to point queries—exclude or split those sourcetypes when noise appears. Training tenants with explicit permission to stress-test cardinality may trigger Loki querier warnings that mirror abuse; confirm org identifiers before paging production owners. Prometheus subqueries with long lookbacks and fine resolution create heavy server work even when analysts act responsibly; teach safer step sizes rather than muting the detection entirely. PostgreSQL or MySQL cold-buffer states after maintenance reboots inflate first-query latency; validate database restart timelines. Splunk realtime searches launched through Grafana are inherently slower than historical batches; do not treat them as Explore storms without quota context. Penetration tests that simulate credential stuffing in Grafana are out of scope here but may add unrelated log volume; gate on Explore-specific paths. Duplicate forwarders that double-ingest the same Grafana file may inflate query_count until deduplication lands; monitor ingest health separately.
